words.example: Whose phone is this?
You should drink at least two liters of water a day.
Do you have any further questions?
Excuse me, ma'am, you dropped your scarf.
This painting is worth a lot of money.
That was a very weird movie.
Please be quiet in the library.
It is quite cold outside today.
I would rather stay home than go out.
She worked hard to achieve her goals.
We went for a walk despite the rain.
It's a fairly common problem.
Read the contract carefully to avoid any gotchas.
Gotcha! I'll be there in five minutes.
Would you like another cup of coffee?
Are you aware of the risks?
Scientists have made a major breakthrough in cancer research.

words.portuguese: meio que, tipo que, uma espécie de
words.example: I'm sort of tired today.

words.portuguese: revisar, retocar, melhorar algo que já sabe
words.example: I need to brush up on my Spanish before the trip.
Stop goofing off and get back to work!

words.portuguese: dar uma olhada, ter um vislumbre
words.example: I only managed to get a glimpse of the singer as he drove by.
I've been working on this for an hour, and so far so good.
Look out! There's a car coming.
The meeting starts at two o'clock.
We spent the day at the beach.
The dog is a bitch, not a male.
It was a tough decision to make.
I like him, though he can be annoying sometimes.
I thought you were coming with us.
We drove through the tunnel.
The doctor gave him a thorough examination.
It rained throughout the night.
I brought a cake for the party.
She bought a new car yesterday.
Follow the golden rule.
He played a major role in the project.
He is claiming to be the owner.
I actually didn't know that.
In fact, I was just about to call you.
Let's walk instead of taking the bus.
I haven't seen him since Monday.
The game was cancelled due to rain.
I think, therefore I am.
The doctor will be here soon; in the meantime, please relax.
Never mind, I'll do it myself.
What a pity that you can't come.
We should be arriving pretty soon.
Hopefully, it won't rain tomorrow.
Are you coming? You bet!
Don't worry about the bill, I got it.
He thinks he's the boss. As if!
I wake up at 7 AM every day.
How come you didn't call me?
I forgot to bring the keys. My bad.
Besides, it's too late to go now.
Don't throw away that box; we might need it.
My parents were very strict about grades.
He is late, as usual.
Stop doing that! I mean it.
Where did they go? I wish I knew.
Can I help you? No thanks, I'm just browsing.
The bill is $40. Let's split it.
I visit my grandparents once in a while.
By the way, did you call Mom?
Hmm, let me think about that for a second.
We can eat pizza or burgers, it's up to you.
Wait for me, I'm on my way!
Dinner at 8? Sounds good.
I'm not mad, just kidding!
I can't wait for summer vacation.
What do you mean by that?

words.portuguese: deixa pra lá, não se preocupe
words.example: You broke the glass? Don't worry about it, it was old.
No rush, take your time.
It's been a while since we last met.

sentences.english: Have you guys ever gone camping?
sentences.portuguese: Vocês já foram acampar?

sentences.english: He gave her a kiss on the cheek
sentences.portuguese: Ele deu um beijo na bochecha dela

sentences.english: Which bowl would you like your ice cream in?
sentences.portuguese: Em qual tigela gostaria do seu sorvete?

sentences.english: Am I coming through ok?
sentences.portuguese: Está me escutando?

sentences.english: People are making fun of me for hugging my friends. They said it's too much.
sentences.portuguese: As pessoas estão tirando sarro de mim por abraçar meus amigos. Dizem que é demais.

sentences.english: And do you want to know something even cooler?
sentences.portuguese: E quer saber algo ainda mais legal?

sentences.english: Have all the students finished reviewing the lesson yet?
sentences.portuguese: Todos os alunos já terminaram de rever a lição?

sentences.english: Have the kids fallen asleep yet?
sentences.portuguese: As crianças já caíram no sono?

sentences.english: Have you ever fallen asleep in class?
sentences.portuguese: Você caiu no sono na aula alguma vez?

sentences.english: Only six weeks' worth of research data
sentences.portuguese: Apenas seis semanas de dados de pesquisa

sentences.english: Don't be a jerk
sentences.portuguese: Não seja um idiota

sentences.english: In Ireland, it's nice to bring flowers when you go to someone's house.
sentences.portuguese: Na Irlanda, é simpático levar flores quando você vai a casa de alguém.

sentences.english: Software Engineering progression I've seen is: Associate;junior;no prefix;senior;staff;senior staff;principal - architect
sentences.portuguese: A progressão que tenho visto na Engenharia de Software é: Associado;júnior;sem prefixo;sênior;equipe;equipe sênior;diretor;arquiteto

sentences.english: It's nice to meet you
sentences.portuguese: Prazer em conhecê-lo

sentences.english: Which sauce would you like the pasta with?
sentences.portuguese: Você gostaria da massa com qual molho?

sentences.english: She's been an engineer for seventeen years.
sentences.portuguese: Ela é engenheira há dezessete anos.

sentences.english: That was the worst headache that I've ever had.
sentences.portuguese: Essa foi a pior dor de cabeça que já tive.

sentences.english: This is the tastiest soup that I've ever had!
sentences.portuguese: Esta é a sopa mais saborosa que já comi!

sentences.english: What a shame!
sentences.portuguese: Que pena!

sentences.english: The king was kind to everyone, even his lazy horse.
sentences.portuguese: O rei era amável com todos, mesmo com o cavalo preguiçoso dele.

sentences.english: The ticket says the bus arrives at nine. But there's a delay, so we won't get there until about nine thirty.
sentences.portuguese: O bilhete diz que o ônibus chega às nove. Mas há um atraso, então não chegaremos lá antes das nove e meia.

sentences.english: It isn't an international flight, so you won't need to show your passport at the airport.
sentences.portuguese: Não é um voo internacional, então você não precisará mostrar seu passaporte no aeroporto.

sentences.english: How long does it take you to get home?
sentences.portuguese: Quanto tempo você demora para chegar em casa?

sentences.english: That's such a shame!
sentences.portuguese: Isso é uma pena e tanto!

sentences.english: People still told stories about dragons, even many years later.
sentences.portuguese: Pessoas ainda contavam histórias sobre dragões, mesmo muitos anos depois.

sentences.english: What kind of bread would you like the ham on?
sentences.portuguese: Em que tipo de pão você gostaria do presunto?

sentences.english: Have you ever tried onion sauce?
sentences.portuguese: Você já experimentou molho de cebola?

sentences.english: There's a no parking sign.
sentences.portuguese: Há uma placa de proíbido estacionar.

sentences.english: You should try on this green sweater. It's going to look good on you.
sentences.portuguese: Você deveria experimentar esse suéter verde. Vai ficar bem em você.

sentences.english: Running an errand. Call when you're up! Love You! Ben.
sentences.portuguese: Fui resolver uma pendência. Ligue quando acordar! Amo você! Ben.

sentences.english: We can look after ourselves.
sentences.portuguese: Nós sabemos nos cuidar.

sentences.english: I'm actually 59 and 6 months I will be 60 and it's kind of weird because 60.
sentences.portuguese: Atualmente tenho 59 anos e daqui a 6 meses faço 60 e é um pouco estranho porque 60.

sentences.english: Was there actually a magic cup somewhere in the east?
sentences.portuguese: Havia realmente um cálice mágico em algum lugar no leste?

sentences.english: Can you look after my cats?
sentences.portuguese: Você pode cuidar dos meus gatos?

sentences.english: When did their airplane take off?
sentences.portuguese: Quando o avião deles levantou voo?

sentences.english: I got an extra dessert for free
sentences.portuguese: Eu recebi uma sobremesa extra de graça

sentences.english: Parking will cost at least five dollars.
sentences.portuguese: O estacionamento custará pelo menos cinco dólares.

sentences.english: Come to us?
sentences.portuguese: Vem buscar a gente?

sentences.english: Ask a tour guide for further information.
sentences.portuguese: Peça mais informações a um guia turístico.

sentences.english: Yes, of course
sentences.portuguese: Sim, é claro

sentences.english: as a matter of fact
sentences.portuguese: na verdade

sentences.english: I've never touched it as a matter of fact I've only read a few things about it like really how it compares to Java
sentences.portuguese: Na verdade, nunca toquei nele, só li algumas coisas sobre ele, como realmente se compara ao Java.

sentences.english: last but not least
sentences.portuguese: por último mas não menos importante

sentences.english: process then try to cram as much as I can in my head and be miserable
sentences.portuguese: processo então tento enfiar o máximo que posso na minha cabeça e fico infeliz

sentences.english: I'll kind of skim through
sentences.portuguese: Vou dar uma olhada rápida

sentences.english: Why has he taken so long to contact you?
sentences.portuguese: Por que ele demorou tanto para contatar você?

sentences.english: Please tell me that this is the end.
sentences.portuguese: Por favor, me diga que isto é o fim.

sentences.english: It's a shame that they'll miss the barbecue
sentences.portuguese: É uma pena que elas perderão o churrasco.

sentences.english: Do you mind bringing a salad?
sentences.portuguese: Você se importa de trazer uma salada?

sentences.english: What grade is she in?
sentences.portuguese: Em que ano ela está?

sentences.english: I have had a stomachache since dinner
sentences.portuguese: Eu estou com dor de estômago desde o jantar.

sentences.english: She ate the cake by herself.
sentences.portuguese: Ela comeu o bolo sozinha.

sentences.english: It's only a shoe store, what are you afraid of?
sentences.portuguese: É só uma loja de sapatos, do que você tem medo?

sentences.english: Where should I get off the train?
sentences.portuguese: Onde eu deveria descer do trem?

sentences.english: I think we're going south
sentences.portuguese: Eu acho que nós estamos indo para o sul

sentences.english: Go straight for a few kilometers
sentences.portuguese: Siga em frente por alguns quilometros.

sentences.english: I'm a rather ordinary person and to me it’s all but earth shattering.
sentences.portuguese: Sou uma pessoa bastante comum e para mim isso é quase algo avassalador.

sentences.english: pretty good name if I do say so myself
sentences.portuguese: nome muito bom se me permite dizer

sentences.english: I've practiced soccer every day since then
sentences.portuguese: Eu tenho treinado futebol todos os dias desde então

sentences.english: Piece of cake, right?
sentences.portuguese: Fácil, né?

sentences.english: Today, I cycled more than 200 kilometers
sentences.portuguese: Hoje pedalei mais de 200 quilômetros

sentences.english: it now if you haven't caught on yet that
sentences.portuguese: Agora se você ainda não percebeu isso

sentences.english: Intro was just a little tongue and cheek
sentences.portuguese: A introdução foi só uma brincadeira

sentences.english: Do you mind
sentences.portuguese: Você se importa

sentences.english: There are going to be so many people at the dance
sentences.portuguese: Vai haver tantas pessoas no baile!

sentences.english: I'll be renting the car by Friday.
sentences.portuguese: Eu alugarei o carro até sexta-feira.

sentences.english: They should eat healthier food, shouldn't they?
sentences.portuguese: Eles deveriam comer comida mais saudável, não deveriam?

sentences.english: I'm going to need a little time to get ready.
sentences.portuguese: Eu vou precisar de um pouquinho de tempo para ficar pronto.

sentences.english: Remember to bring a little cash.
sentences.portuguese: Lembre de trazer um pouquinho de dinheiro.

sentences.english: We'll be having the barbecue in the front yard.
sentences.portuguese: Nós faremos o churrasco no quintal da frente.

sentences.english: He dried the dishes with that towel.
sentences.portuguese: Ele secou a louça com aquela toalha.

sentences.english: Ok, let me know!
sentences.portuguese: Certo, avise-me

sentences.english: Do you mind shutting the door?
sentences.portuguese: Você se importa de fechar a porta?

sentences.english: I've never taken care of my teeth.
sentences.portuguese: Eu nunca cuidei dos meus dentes.

sentences.english: Could I have a piece of that apple?
sentences.portuguese: Você poderia me dar um pedaço dessa maçã?

sentences.english: I never feel like cleaning the apartment!
sentences.portuguese: Eu nunca tenho vontade de limpar o apartamento.

sentences.english: Has she ever gone skateboarding at that park?
sentences.portuguese: Ela já andou de skate naquele parque alguma vez?

sentences.english: Who's the boss talking to?
sentences.portuguese: Com quem a chefe está falando?

sentences.english: They might ask us to get there early.
sentences.portuguese: Pode ser que elas nos peçam para chegar lá cedo.

sentences.english: I'll be renting the car till Friday.
sentences.portuguese: Eu alugarei o carro até sexta-feira.

sentences.english: They will be running along the beach.
sentences.portuguese: Eles correrão ao longo da praia

sentences.english: They will run along the beach.
sentences.portuguese: Eles correrão pela praia.

sentences.english: That’s awesome! I’m sure you’ll do great. What will you pair it with?
sentences.portuguese: Isso é incrível! Tenho certeza de que você vai se sair muito bem. Com o que você vai combiná-lo?

sentences.english: How long will the ceremony last?
sentences.portuguese: Quanto tempo vai durar a cerimônia?

sentences.english: I’m not sure about that
sentences.portuguese: Permite discordar sem conflito e com segurança.

sentences.english: That’s exactly what I meant
sentences.portuguese: Confirma ideias de forma clara e natural.

sentences.english: Let me check on that
sentences.portuguese: Muito usada no trabalho e no dia a dia.

sentences.english: As far as I know
sentences.portuguese: Ate onde eu sei

sentences.english: I’ll get back to you
sentences.portuguese: Garante continuidade sem pressão imediata.

sentences.english: That works for me
sentences.portuguese: Resposta comum em decisões simples.

sentences.english: I didn’t catch that
sentences.portuguese: Forma natural de mostrar que algo passou despercebido.

sentences.english: I agree with you
sentences.portuguese: Posicionamento direto e respeitoso.

sentences.english: Let’s move on
sentences.portuguese: Ajuda a avançar conversas sem desconforto.

sentences.english: I’m still learning
sentences.portuguese: Reduz pressão interna e mantém diálogo aberto.

sentences.english: Relax. Let me handle this. I've fought bigger things.
sentences.portuguese: Calma. Deixe me cuidar disso. Eu já lutei com coisas maiores.

sentences.english: Lin grabs a broom and Vikram picks up a pan. They slowly approach the door.
sentences.portuguese: Lin pega uma vassoura e Vikram pega uma panela. Eles lentamente se aproximam da porta.

sentences.english: My sister will be looking after the kids
sentences.portuguese: Minha irmã cuidará das crianças

sentences.english: Look out, you're too close to the candle
sentences.portuguese: Tenha cuidado, você está perto demais da vela!

sentences.english: Besides fishing, the prince also enjoys sailing
sentences.portuguese: Além de pesca, o principe também curte navegação a vela

sentences.english: The ceremony lasted about two hours.
sentences.portuguese: A cerimônia durou cerca de duas horas

sentences.english: One of my relatives is a well-known musician.
sentences.portuguese: Um dos meus parentes é um músico bem-conhecido

sentences.english: The movie will be over soon
sentences.portuguese: O filme acabará em breve

sentences.english: Weren't you bored when you were at the mall?
sentences.portuguese: Você não estava entediado quando você estava no shopping?

sentences.english: You weren't even living here when I got rid of it
sentences.portuguese: Você nem morava aqui quando eu me livrei disso

sentences.english: She threw away my sweater, so I need a new one
sentences.portuguese: Ela pôs no lixo meu suéter, então eu preciso de um novo
